commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: refactor(ai-chat): extract ServerSideEventEmitter to make stream_handler testable
commits.body:
Separate SSE I/O (headers, echo, flush) from stream orchestration logic.
stream_handler becomes a thin shell that delegates to handle_stream,
which is tested via FakeServerSideEventEmitter.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: test(ai-chat): add unit tests for AiRoutes generate_ai_response endpoint
commits.body:
Cover request parsing (missing/invalid messages key), happy path
response shaping, and error forwarding through the route layer.

removes all custom media definition related feature flags so the feature
is on all the time

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: test(ai-chat): decouple AiService test from real OpenAiProvider
commits.body:
Use FakeAiProvider instead of OpenAiProvider for the empty-array
validation test so it doesn't break if OpenAiProvider's constructor
changes.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: refactor(ai-chat): extract HTTP transport and response parsing from OpenAiProvider
commits.body:
Make OpenAiProvider testable through its public API by introducing an
HttpTransport interface with a FakeHttpTransport test double, and
extracting OpenAiResponseParser and OpenAiPayloadBuilder into dedicated
classes. 13 tests cover provide_ai_response and stream_ai_response
end-to-end without real HTTP calls.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: refactor(ai-chat): extract OpenAiStreamEventProcessor from OpenAiProvider
commits.body:
Move event routing logic into a dedicated class with a public API,
replacing Reflection-based tests with direct unit tests.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: test(ai-chat): add acceptance-criteria tests for AiService using FakeAiProvider
commits.body:
Cover streamed deltas, conversation history forwarding, whitespace
validation, provider error surfacing, and validation short-circuiting.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: refactor(ai-chat): separate HTTP concerns from business logic
commits.body:
Move message validation, feature flag gating, and reasoning callback
translation from AiRoutes into AiService. AiRoutes now only handles
request deserialization, SSE headers/transport, and 1:1 callback-to-event
mapping.

commits.author: Nuno Ferreira
commits.subject: feat(ai-chat): stream AI responses via Server-Sent Events (#256)
commits.body:
# Summary
- Replaces the single JSON response AI chat endpoint with real-time SSE
streaming (`/ai-chat/stream`), delivering deltas as they arrive from
OpenAI
- Adds a typed `ServerSideEventsParser` (frontend) and
`ServerSideEventsFramesParser` (PHP) to parse and validate SSE frames
with Zod schemas
- Extracts `MessageUpdater` to handle appending content, reasoning
blocks, and error messages to the assistant message
- Adds chunk-level and total-stream timeout protection with abort
support
- Gates reasoning feature behind `ENABLE_AI_REASONING` flag (`off` by
default)

# Test plan
- [x] `ServerSideEventsParser` unit tests cover parsing, buffering,
event validation, and invalid input
- [x] `ServerSideEventsFramesParser` PHP tests cover frame extraction,
multiline data, CRLF handling
- [x] `AiChatStore` tests cover streaming deltas, reasoning events,
error events, abort, and chunk timeout

---------

Co-authored-by: Matteo Greco <matteo@matteogreco.net>
Co-authored-by: Matteo Greco <matteo-greco@users.noreply.github.com>

commits.author: Christoph Sanz
commits.subject: fix: custom media stylesheet not created until reload and to-rem not substituted in custom media def (#244)
commits.body:
Seems like this is an issue when the setting is set to on, without then
reloading the page. Because the `@custom-media` Stylesheet is created in
php and is dependend on that setting, it will only be created on reload.
If its not there it loads the default stylesheet which is the first one.

I fixed it by creating the `@custom-media` stylesheet
regardless of having the setting on.

It also fixes the to-rem conversion in `@custom-media` definitions.

commits.author: Reinhard Sanz
commits.subject: feat(POC): Group prop (#253)
commits.body:
Very very first part of group prop. By no means releasable!

It can be enabled with the "ENABLE_GROUP_PROP" feature flag.

For it to actually be releasable a few things need to be implemented. 
Mainly proper resolving, collision handling and much more.

Releasing this as is would just result in user complaints about missing
features, that we are aware of though and actively working.
E.g. Why can't i add a boolean in the group? -> Answer: Obviously we are
gonna add support for that.

The user can "add" a text prop but it's not usable right now.

---------

Co-authored-by: Andrea Maccone <andrea@digitalgravy.co>
